back_translation: When Mordecai learned all that had happened, he tore his clothes, put on sackcloth and covered himself with ashes, and went out into the middle of the city, wailing loudly and bitterly.
He came only as far as the palace gate, because there was a law forbidding anyone wearing sackcloth to enter the palace gate.
In every province where the king's command and edict reached, there was great mourning among the Jews, with fasting, weeping, and lamenting; many lay in sackcloth and ashes.
When Esther's maids and eunuchs came and told her about this, the queen was greatly distressed, so she sent clothes for Mordecai to wear instead of his sackcloth, but he would not accept them.
So Queen Esther summoned Hathach, one of the king's eunuchs appointed to attend her, and ordered him to go to Mordecai to find out what was happening and why.
Hathach went out to Mordecai at the city square in front of the palace gate.
Mordecai told him everything that had happened to him, including the exact sum of money that Haman had promised to weigh into the royal treasury in exchange for destroying the Jews.

He also gave Hathach a copy of the decree issued in Susa to destroy the Jews, to show Queen Esther and explain it to her, and to charge her to go before the king to plead for mercy and entreat on behalf of her people.
Hathach returned and reported Mordecai's words to Queen Esther.
Queen Esther spoke to Hathach and instructed him to tell Mordecai,

'All the king's servants and the people of the king's provinces know that for any man or woman who goes to the king in the inner court without being summoned, there is only one law — that they be put to death — unless the king holds out the gold scepter, that they may live. And I have not been summoned to go to the king for thirty days now.'
When Esther's words were reported to Mordecai,
Mordecai sent back a reply to Queen Esther, 'Do not think that you will escape, more than all the Jews, just because you are in the king's palace.

For if you keep silent at this time, relief and deliverance for the Jews will arise from another place, but you and your father's house will perish. And who knows whether perhaps you have come to your royal position for a time such as this?'
Then Queen Esther replied to Mordecai,

'Go, gather all the Jews who are in Susa, and fast for me; do not eat or drink for three days, night and day. I and my maids will fast likewise. Then I will go to the king, though it is against the law; and if I perish, I perish.'
So Mordecai went and did everything that Queen Esther had commanded him.
